rationale: The empty set omits everything the statement rests on. Alpha-equivalence is asserted as a logical equivalence (==, def:log-equiv) between a formula and its bound-variable rename phi[y/x] (def:subst-notation), under the side condition 'y not free in phi' (def:bound-free). All three are load-bearing.
notes: Definitional twin of thm:rename (0041), which carries def:free-for + def:subst-notation; here the equivalence framing makes def:log-equiv primary. def:subst-notation licensed by 'phi[y/x]'; def:bound-free by 'provided y is not free in phi'. The quantifier forms are the ambient setting.
